title: Chicken Salami & Ham Roll
tagline: Pan-crisped chicken salami and ham rolled up with melty cheese and sauce.
macroNotes: Estimated per roll: chicken salami + chicken ham + 1 slice cheese + 1 paratha/tortilla + sauce and veg.
Salami and ham are processed and fairly salty/fatty — good protein but watch the sodium. Going easy on the cheese and sauce keeps it in check.
Protein boost: add a fried or scrambled egg inside each roll for ~6 g more protein, or use a high-protein wrap. Soya doesn't fit this dish.

items: 1 tsp oil, for cooking the meats
Sauce of choice: mayo, mint mayo, peri-peri or ketchup + mustard

steps.title: Cook the meats first
steps.body: Even though the salami and ham are pre-cooked, cook them for flavour and safety — the ham packet says 2 minutes. Drizzle 1 tsp oil in a non-stick pan on medium, add the 6 salami and 4 ham slices and cook about 1 minute per side until slightly golden and sizzling. This crisps the edges and makes them taste much better than cold from the pack.

steps.title: If frozen
steps.body: If the salami is straight from the freezer, thaw it first: lay slices on a plate, cover with a damp paper towel and microwave in 30-second bursts at medium power, flipping once (1–2 minutes total), until hot through with no cold center. Then still pan-crisp it for texture.

steps.title: Add egg (optional)
steps.body: For extra protein, push the meat aside and scramble or fry 2 eggs in the same pan, or make a thin omelette to layer into the rolls.

steps.title: Melt the cheese
steps.body: Lay 2 slices of cheese over the hot meats in the pan for the last 30 seconds so they start to melt and bind everything.

steps.title: Warm the wrap
steps.body: Warm 2 parathas/rotis/tortillas in a dry pan for 20–30 seconds a side so they're pliable and don't crack when rolled.

steps.title: Build and roll
steps.body: Down the middle of each wrap layer the crisped salami, ham, melty cheese, a little sliced onion, tomato and lettuce/cabbage, and a line of your sauce (mayo, mint mayo or peri-peri). Roll it up tight.

steps.title: Toast the roll
steps.body: Set the rolls seam-side down in the pan and lightly toast on medium until golden and crisp on the outside and the cheese is fully melted inside. Slice in half and eat hot.

notes: Works just as well as a sandwich if you have bread — the golden crisp outside with melted cheese and sauce balances the salty meat well.
Level it up with sautéed onions or bell peppers inside, or swap in a mint-mayo or peri-peri sauce.
Cooking the salami and ham before rolling is the key step — don't skip it, cold-from-pack is much blander.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
